title: dicranidae
intro: The rank Dicranidae is a subfamily classification in the taxonomy of plants, referring to a group of mosses that are part of the Dicranales order. This subfamily is characterized by plants with a single stem (monopodial) or multiple stems (sympodial) arising from the base, often with leaves that are flat or strap-shaped. Examples of orders that include Dicranidae subfamilies include Asparagales (containing the asparagus plant), Malpighiales (containing the maple and hibiscus plants), and Poales (containing the grasses and sedges). Dicranidae subfamilies can be found in a variety of habitats, including wetlands, forests, and mountainous regions.

orders.order_desc: Dicranales is an order of mosses within the class Bryopsida.
